docs.title: Passmark TestOps Guide
docs.subtitle: A practical system guide for new users to understand the product purpose, operating flow, run queue, API, setup, and development guardrails.
docs.purposeText: Passmark TestOps is a local AI Automation Studio that helps QC/Testers create projects, manage targets, manage suites/cases, and run automation tests through the UI instead of writing Playwright manually.
docs.corePrincipleText: The main flow now uses Custom suites: the backend gives AI a QA lead role, asks for many focused cases, returns the explanation, and renders a safe Playwright spec.
docs.backendItemServer: Node.js + TypeScript, native HTTP server in
docs.backendItemDb: Prisma is the ORM, PostgreSQL is the primary database.
docs.backendItemAi: The local AI client lives in
docs.backendItemQueue: The run worker uses an in-memory queue, default concurrency is 1.
docs.frontendItemI18n: Dark dashboard UI, VI/EN support through
docs.frontendItemApi: The frontend calls backend APIs and never calls local AI directly.
docs.userStepProject: Create Project: enter name, environment, and default Base URL.
docs.userStepTarget: Create Test Target: declare what to test, such as web-url, local-web, source-code, or api.
docs.userStepSuite: Create Test Suite: choose the suite type; the current main flow is Custom.
docs.userStepCase: Manage Test Case Library: enable/disable cases and edit description, priority, and expected result.
docs.userStepRun: Run test: choose project, suite, target, enter an AI request if needed, then click Run test.
docs.userStepResult: Review result: follow queued/running state, Latest Result, AI Rendered Flow, Playwright code, and Run History.
docs.targetWebPurpose: Test a public or staging website.
docs.targetLocalPurpose: Test a local web app running on the machine.
docs.targetApiPurpose: Store an endpoint/base URL for a later API testing phase.
docs.targetSourcePurpose: Store a source path for a later source scan phase.
docs.targetFallbackPrefix: If no target is selected when running a test, the system falls back to
docs.targetFallbackSuffix: so the old flow remains compatible.
docs.runStepPoll: Frontend shows queued/running status and polls
docs.runStepWorkerSuffix: calls AI when needed, renders the spec, and runs Playwright Chromium.
docs.restartPrefix: When the backend starts again, old runs still in
docs.seoEnabledText: The run flow executes only enabled test cases. SEO Basic still keeps all enabled cases from the case library and does not let AI cut them down.
docs.ruleApi: Do not change API response format unless truly needed.
docs.ruleFrontendAi: Do not let the frontend call local AI directly.
docs.ruleEnv: Do not hardcode AI URL/key/model in source code; always read them from
docs.ruleSqlite: Do not return to SQLite for the main flow; SQLite is legacy migration only.
docs.ruleNoTs: Do not let AI create dangerous, destructive, stress, or DDoS tests.
docs.ruleRenderer: Keep the template renderer as the stable source of Playwright code.
docs.ruleQueue: The current queue is in-memory and designed for a future Redis/BullMQ upgrade.
docs.troubleDockerTextSuffix: open Docker Desktop first, then run
docs.troublePrismaText: Stop any server/node process that is holding the Prisma engine, then run
docs.troublePlaywrightTextSuffix: if the machine does not have the Chromium runtime yet.
docs.roadmapApi: API testing runner for target type
docs.roadmapUser: Multi-user support, roles, and audit log.
docs.roadmapReport: PDF/DOCX report export and defect trend dashboard.
